examine_details.cable_anchor: A massive steel anchor point where a suspension cable meets the deck. The bolts are rusted. One is missing entirely.
examine_details.inspection_tag: A small metal tag wired to the cable: LAST INSPECTED — 2019. It's been seven years.

urban_disaster+construction_failure.name: Apartment Lobby
urban_disaster+construction_failure.description: A dated lobby with fading floral wallpaper and a row of brass mailboxes. A small elevator sits with its doors open.

examine_details.lobby_ceiling: A long, jagged crack runs from the front door all the way to the elevator. Plaster dust occasionally drifts down from it.
examine_details.mailboxes: Stuffed with junk mail. The brass is tarnished.
examine_details.elevator: An old cage elevator. It groans audibly, even when empty.

base.name: Side Street
base.description: A quieter side street. Less traffic. A few parked cars.

structural_collapse+mechanical_failure.name: River Bank — Under the Bridge
structural_collapse+mechanical_failure.description: A muddy path along the river bank, directly beneath the bridge. The underside of the deck is visible — steel beams, rust stains, pigeon nests. Water drips from somewhere above.

examine_details.bridge_underside: From below, the bridge looks different. The steel beams are streaked with rust. Chunks of concrete have spalled off, exposing rebar. This bridge is sick.
examine_details.support_column: A massive concrete support column. A crack runs vertically from the waterline to the deck. It's been patched with what looks like... duct tape and expanding foam.
examine_details.rust_stains: Orange-brown streaks run down the concrete like tears. The steel is weeping.

urban_disaster+construction_failure.name: Alleyway
urban_disaster+construction_failure.description: A narrow alley between two apartment buildings. Fire escapes zigzag down the brick walls.

examine_details.brick_wall: The exterior load-bearing wall. It's noticeably bowing outward near the bottom.
examine_details.fire_escape: Rusted metal stairs clinging to the side of the building. They look like they'd rip out of the masonry if more than two people stood on them.

base.name: Interior
base.description: Inside the building. The walls, the floor, the ceiling — all solid. All reliable. All taken for granted.

structural_collapse+mechanical_failure.name: Bridge West Side — Traffic Stop
structural_collapse+mechanical_failure.description: The west end of the bridge. Traffic has stopped — a fender bender up ahead. Horns blare. The bridge vibrates with the weight of stationary vehicles. You can feel a rhythmic swaying that wasn't there before.

examine_details.stopped_traffic: Vehicles packed bumper to bumper. Nobody can move. The school bus is right in the middle of the span.
examine_details.bridge_sway: The bridge is swaying. Not from wind — from weight. The rhythm is wrong. It's getting worse.
examine_details.cracking_sound: A deep, structural groan. Like the sound a frozen lake makes before it gives way. But this isn't ice. This is steel and concrete.

urban_disaster+construction_failure.name: First Floor Hallway
urban_disaster+construction_failure.description: A long, dimly lit corridor. The doors to the apartments are all shut. The floor is slanting noticeably to the left.

examine_details.slanting_floor: If you put a marble down here, it would roll into the wall with frightening speed.
examine_details.door_frames: None of the doors sit square in their frames anymore. The building is actively settling.

base.name: Upper Level
base.description: Higher ground. A view of what's below. A view of what's coming.
base.first_entry_text: From up here, you can see everything.

urban_disaster+geological_event.name: Business District — Alleyway
urban_disaster+geological_event.description: You ducked into an alleyway between two high-rises. The asphalt beneath your feet feels soft. Unanchored.
urban_disaster+geological_event.first_entry_text:
[color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] grabbed you by the shirt and hurled you backward into the alleyway just as the street ahead groaned.

The street didn't crack. It just... dropped.

A perfectly circular hole, eighty feet across, swallowed the intersection. A city bus fell straight down into the darkness. The sound of the subterranean water main rushing into the void echoed up the sides.

The edges of the sinkhole are still crumbling, taking sidewalks, streetlights, and pedestrians with them into the abyss.

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color][color=#BC13FE] {visionary}[/color] wasn't kidding.

examine_details.sinkhole_edge: The asphalt is shearing off in massive chunks. You can hear the water rushing far below.
examine_details.falling_debris: A parking meter slides into the hole, disappearing without a sound.

structural_collapse.name: East Bank Overlook
structural_collapse.description: You made it off the bridge. You're standing on the east bank, looking back at the span. The swaying is visible now — a slow pendulum motion that bridges are not supposed to make.
structural_collapse.first_entry_text:
[color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] physically pulled you off the bridge.

You turn around when you hear it — the first sounds of metal.

The centre span drops first. Not a gradual failure — a sudden, sickening lurch downward, like a trapdoor releasing. Cables snap like guitar strings, each one a separate rifle crack.

The sound is a screeching barrage of metal on asphalt and then metal on water and then silence, which is worse.

The bridge folds in half. Vehicles slide into the gap. A school bus. A delivery truck. A car with its hazards on that had been stopped in traffic.

The river swallows everything.

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color][color=#BC13FE] {visionary}[/color] was right.

examine_details.collapsed_span: The centre span of the {city_name} Memorial Bridge hangs vertically, submerged to its midpoint. Suspension cables trail in the current like dead vines. The span is still moving — swaying in the current.
examine_details.river_debris: Vehicles are visible just below the surface. One pair of taillights is still on, blinking red in the murk. Emergency sirens layer over each other from both banks.

urban_disaster+flood_related.name: Parking Structure — Level Three
urban_disaster+flood_related.description: The open third level of the parking structure. You came up here to get away from the street. The rain hit like a wall five minutes ago and hasn't stopped.
urban_disaster+flood_related.first_entry_text:
The storm drain on the corner of 4th and Main failed with a [color=#aaaaaa]sickening, subterranean vomit[/color]. You watched from the sidewalk as the street inverted—the water erupting [color=#55aaff]upward[/color] in a geyser of silt and sewage instead of draining away.

You scrambled up the concrete stairs of the parking structure, following the [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] as they clawed their way toward the upper decks. You felt like a fool—your car was parked on level one—but the [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] wouldn't stop screaming about [color=#FF3131]a flash flood that was coming.[/color]

In minutes, the downtown corridor is transformed into a [color=#55aaff]roaring, debris-choked canyon[/color]. It isn't just a flood; it’s a high-velocity river with a [color=#FF3131]killing current[/color] fast enough to tumble parked SUVs like [color=#aaaaaa]scrap metal[/color]. The water hits a blown transformer box on the corner. The blue flash arcs across the surface of the water, [color=#3399ff]electrocuting[/color] everyone still trying to swim.

The traffic signals are still cycling with [color=#aaaaaa]blind, robotic indifference[/color]. Green. Yellow. Red. Reflecting off a surface that is now [color=#FF3131]thick with gasoline and wreckage[/color].

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] God... [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] felt the pressure in the pipes before the first manhole cover blew. You were supposed to be [color=#FF3131]drowned and crushed[/color] in that lower deck.

examine_details.flood_street_view: The entire block is underwater. The water line is at the top of the parking meters — about four feet. It's still rising. The current is carrying debris: bins, tyres, a push-bike.
examine_details.drainage_failure: You can see where the storm drain gave out — a geyser of brown water is still shooting up from the grate like a fountain. The city's drainage system has reversed.

construction_failure.name: Opposite Pavement — Doorway
construction_failure.description: The doorway of the corner shop across the street. You ducked in here. The building opposite — your building — has been making a sound for the last thirty seconds.
construction_failure.first_entry_text:
The sound was [color=#aaaaaa]subterranean[/color]. A deep, rhythmic groaning from the foundation was transmitted through the pavement and into the marrow of your bones. [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] didn't just warn you; they [color=#FF3131]seized you by the throat[/color] and dragged you across the asphalt, shoving you into a brick doorway just as the air began to [color=#aaaaaa]vibrate[/color].

The building doesn't collapse dramatically; it collapses [color=#aaaaaa]practically[/color]. You watch with a hollow chest as the floors [color=#FF3131]pancake in sequence[/color], a mechanical heartbeat of stone-on-stone. Each level adds its mass to the next, the whole structure [color=#FF3131]compressing downward[/color] with a slow, deliberate gravity that [color=#aaaaaa]unzips the support columns[/color] like dry brittle.

The air trapped inside the lobby has nowhere to go. As the weight descends, the ground floor windows [color=#FF3131]detonate outward[/color] from the sheer atmospheric pressure. A [color=#aaaaaa]hurricane of jagged glass[/color] shrieks across the street, moving with the velocity of shrapnel. One jagged shard—eight inches of razor-sharp plate—slams into the door frame [color=#FF3131]inches from your temple[/color], vibrating with a high, singing note.

A [color=#aaaaaa]silt-heavy dust cloud[/color] the size of a city block exhales from the ruins, smelling of [color=#aaaaaa]pulverized concrete and old insulation[/color].

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] felt the concrete fatigue before the first rebar snapped. You were supposed to be [color=#FF3131]compacted[/color] between those floors.

examine_details.rubble_mound: The building is now approximately twelve feet tall where it was six stories. A stairwell column is still standing — the rest is a uniform grey mound. You can hear something from inside it. Something alive.
examine_details.glass_scatter: Glass covers the entire pavement on both sides of the street. Some pieces are still falling from the floors that haven't fully settled.

natural_disaster+structural_collapse.name: Rooftop Terrace — Overlook
natural_disaster+structural_collapse.description: The rooftop terrace of your highrise building. From up here, you have a terrifying, unobstructed view of the ruined city grid below.
natural_disaster+structural_collapse.first_entry_text:
You chased [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] up the emergency stairwell to the roof, screaming for them to make sense, as they burst through the terrace doors rasping about [color=#FF3131]'concrete rolling like waves'[/color]

Car alarms triggered all at once—a frantic, [color=#aaaaaa]unison of terror[/color] echoing off the downtown highrises. Then came the P-wave. It was a rolling, almost [color=#55aaff]nauseating sensation[/color], the entire skyscraper swaying under your feet like a slow boat in deep swells. You looked down; the coffee in your hand was rippling in perfect, [color=#aaaaaa]concentric circles[/color] of doom.

The S-wave hit four seconds later, and the metro grid became a [color=#FF3131]meat-grinder[/color].

The historic masonry building across the avenue—six floors of heavy brick and mortar—didn't just crumble. In the distance, the suspension bridge violently whipped like a jump rope as the city's foundation [color=#aaaaaa]liquefied[/color]. The brick building tilted sideways with the slow, ponderous grace of a dying giant. You watched from above as the structural seams [color=#FF3131]erupted in a spray of red dust[/color] before the entire mass slammed into the asphalt, the impact [color=#FF3131]shattering[/color] the windows of every car on the block.

The highrise is still groaning. The earth has been moving for thirty seconds, a [color=#aaaaaa]low, rhythmic growl[/color] that sounds like the planet's own teeth grinding together. It doesn't stop. It just [color=#FF3131]keeps moving[/color].

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] God... [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] felt the fault line snap before the first foundation shifted. You were supposed to be [color=#FF3131]pulverized[/color] down there under those floors of brick.

examine_details.ruined_avenue: Half the buildings below are severely damaged, their facades sheared off to reveal the ruined apartments inside. Gas is burning somewhere two streets over. You can hear the fire roaring above the distant sirens.
examine_details.fractured_parapet: The concrete safety wall at the edge of the roof has cracked straight through. The fissure is four inches wide, a terrifying reminder of the sheer stress this skyscraper just endured.

drowning_water+transportation_water.name: Lakeshore Promenade — North Pier
drowning_water+transportation_water.description: The north pier of the promenade. You watched the Queen Isabella pull away from here. You almost didn't get off in time.
drowning_water+transportation_water.first_entry_text:
You were the last one down the gangway, shoved into the air by a panicked, [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] whose hands were [color=#FF3131]trembling with a violent, animal heat[/color]. The deckhand was already unhooking the chains when your boots hit the pier.

You stood on the concrete and watched the [color=#aaaaaa]Queen Isabella[/color] groan as she made her turn into the black water. The lights were a [color=#FF3131]blinding, festive gold[/color] across all five decks, casting long, shimmering needles onto the lake.

The list started with a [color=#aaaaaa]low, structural moan[/color]—a degree, then two. It looked almost graceful, until the [color=#aaaaaa]rattle of sliding furniture[/color] began to echo across the water. Through the portholes, you see tiny glints of silver and porcelain [color=#FF3131]tumbling into the abyss[/color] as the dining room floors become walls.

By fifteen degrees, the [color=#FF3131]screaming[/color] reached the shore—a thin, collective wail that was abruptly drowned out by the [color=#aaaaaa]shriek of unzipping rivets[/color]. 

The [color=#aaaaaa]Queen Isabella[/color] rolled past the point of recovery and simply [color=#FF3131]surrendered[/color]. The massive funnel hit the water first with a [color=#FF3131]hiss of escaping steam and boiling blood[/color], before the entire hull came over on top of the lifeboats, [color=#FF3131]snuffing out the lights[/color] and the voices in one final, [color=#aaaaaa]heavy gulp[/color].

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] God... [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] felt the center of gravity shift before the first rope was even loosed. You were supposed to be [color=#FF3131]sealed in that steel tomb[/color].

examine_details.capsized_hull: The hull of the Queen Isabella is visible above the waterline — red anti-fouling paint against black water. It's still moving, slowly rotating as it settles.
examine_details.flotation_debris: The lake surface between the pier and the wreck is covered in life rings, deck chairs, luggage, and people. Rescue boats are launching from the south pier.

outdoor._description: Nature path: trailhead/arrival → open area → viewpoint/shelter → high ground. Covers wildfires, floods, earthquakes, typhoons, ski lifts, sinkholes, dam breaks.

base.name: Arrival Point
base.description: The starting point. A parking lot, a trailhead, a dock. The edge of civilization.
base.first_entry_text:
You're outside. The air is fresh. Nothing bad happens outdoors. Right?

Then[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]started screaming.

car_radio.name: Car Radio
car_radio.description: A battered car nearby is blaring their radio for all to hear.

natural_disaster.name: {city_name} Overlook — Parking Area
natural_disaster.description: A gravel parking lot at the McKinley scenic overlook. A few cars. A ranger station with a CLOSED sign. The sky has an odd color — yellow-grey, like a bruise.
natural_disaster.first_entry_text:
[color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] ripped you out of the car, screaming something about the sky.

The sky looks wrong. The air tastes metallic. The birds are gone — completely silent.

examine_details.sky_color: The sky is the color of a old bruise — yellow, grey, with veins of green. You've never seen a sky like this.
examine_details.silent_trees: The forest is absolutely silent. No birds. No insects. No wind in the leaves. It's like nature is holding its breath.
examine_details.weather_station: A small automated weather station. The wind speed dial is pinned. The barometer is dropping so fast you can watch the needle move.

infrastructure_failure+flood_related.name: {city_name} Dam — Visitor Parking
infrastructure_failure+flood_related.description: A gravel parking lot overlooking the massive concrete wall of the dam. The sky looks bruised, and the air is heavy.
infrastructure_failure+flood_related.first_entry_text: [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] dragged you out of the car, eyes wide, pointing at the concrete face of the dam...

examine_details.dam_wall: A colossal slab of concrete holding back millions of tons of water. You notice a damp patch near the base that wasn't there before.
examine_details.sky_color: The sky is the color of an old bruise...
examine_details.weather_station: The barometer is dropping so fast you can watch the needle move.

winter_sports+mechanical_failure.name: McKinley Peak — Base Lodge
winter_sports+mechanical_failure.description: The base lodge of McKinley Peak ski resort. Skiers and boarders mill about in colorful gear. The lift line stretches across the snow. Gondolas swing overhead on cables.
winter_sports+mechanical_failure.first_entry_text:
Perfect conditions — fresh powder, blue sky, light wind. The mountain is packed.

Then[color=#BC13FE] {visionary} [/color]threw their skis down in the lift line and started shouting that the cables were going to snap. People are staring.

examine_details.lift_line: A queue of skiers shuffles forward. Kids, families, a few show-offs doing tricks in line. Nobody's worried.
examine_details.gondola_cables: Thick steel cables run from the base station up the mountain, disappearing into the trees. The gondolas sway gently. One of them makes a grinding sound as it passes the first tower.
examine_details.conditions_board: CONDITIONS: POWDER — 8" NEW. WIND: 15 MPH GUSTING TO 35. AVALANCHE RISK: MODERATE. Someone taped a note under it: 'Lift 3 cable tension check OVERDUE — see maintenance.'

base.name: Open Area
base.description: A wide open space. Exposed. Nowhere to hide if things go wrong.

natural_disaster.name: {city_name} Overlook — Trail
natural_disaster.description: A dirt trail through sparse trees. The overlook point is ahead. The ground feels unstable — soft, almost spongy. Cracks have appeared in the trail surface.

examine_details.ground_cracks: Fresh cracks in the packed dirt. Some are an inch wide. They weren't here last week — you can tell because the exposed earth is dark and moist.
examine_details.leaning_trees: Several trees are tilted at odd angles. Their roots are partially exposed. The ground shifted under them.
examine_details.distant_rumble: A low rumble from somewhere underground. Not thunder. Deeper. Constant.

winter_sports+mechanical_failure.name: {city_name} Peak — Lift Loading Zone
winter_sports+mechanical_failure.description: The loading zone for Lift 3. Skiers shuffle forward, grabbing chairs as they swing around. An attendant waves people into position. The chairs climb steeply into the trees.

examine_details.lift_chairs: Two-person chairs, swinging around the bullwheel. They look sturdy enough. But one of them has a safety bar that doesn't latch.
examine_details.loading_platform: A rubber mat worn smooth from boots. The attendant looks bored. He hasn't checked the cable monitor in at least an hour.
examine_details.cable_tension_monitor: A small screen showing cable tension readings. The left column reads NOMINAL. The right column reads ALERT — TENSION VARIANCE 12%. Normal variance is under 3%.

base.name: Shelter
base.description: A structure offering some protection from the elements.

natural_disaster.name: {city_name} Overlook — Ranger Cabin
natural_disaster.description: A small ranger cabin, locked and boarded. A radio antenna on the roof sways in wind that wasn't there five minutes ago. An emergency supply box is mounted on the exterior wall.

examine_details.radio_antenna: The antenna whips back and forth. The wind is picking up fast — and it's coming from the wrong direction.
examine_details.emergency_map: A faded evacuation map under cracked plexiglass. EVACUATION ROUTE: FOLLOW TRAIL SOUTH TO PARKING AREA. IN CASE OF EARTHQUAKE: MOVE TO OPEN GROUND.
examine_details.seismograph_printout: A curl of paper from a portable seismograph, still printing. The line is no longer flat. It's spiking. Rhythmically. Getting bigger.

winter_sports+mechanical_failure.name: {city_name} Peak — Ski Patrol Hut
winter_sports+mechanical_failure.description: A small red hut with a white cross. Ski patrol. A stretcher leans against the wall. A radio crackles inside with chatter about 'Lift 3 anomalies.'

examine_details.patrol_radio: 'Control, this is Patrol 7. We're getting reports of unusual vibration on Lift 3. Passengers are complaining. Over.' Static. No response.
examine_details.incident_log: A clipboard. The last three entries are all about Lift 3: 'Cable tension variance.' 'Grinding sound at Tower 4.' 'Gondola #17 safety latch failure.' All from today.
examine_details.mountain_map: A topographic map of McKinley Peak. Someone has drawn a red circle around Tower 4 of Lift 3 and written 'CHECK IMMEDIATELY' in block letters.

base.name: Viewpoint
base.description: The highest accessible point. A panoramic view of everything below.

natural_disaster.name: {city_name} Overlook — Cliff Edge
natural_disaster.description: The scenic overlook. A waist-high railing. Below, the valley spreads out — {city_name}, the river, the downtown core. The ground vibrates under your feet.

examine_details.valley_view: {city_name} sprawls below. From here it looks peaceful. Orderly. Fragile.
examine_details.vibrating_ground: The ground is vibrating. Not shaking — vibrating. A constant, low-frequency hum. The railing buzzes against your palms.
examine_details.railing: A metal safety railing. The bolts anchoring it to the rock are loose. You can wiggle them with your fingers.

winter_sports+mechanical_failure.name: {city_name} Peak — Mid-Station Platform
winter_sports+mechanical_failure.description: The mid-station where Lift 3 passes through a support structure. From here you can see the cable running up to the summit and back down to the base. Gondolas full of skiers slide past.

examine_details.cable_closeup: The cable passes inches from the platform. You can hear it — a low singing sound, like a guitar string under too much tension. The pitch is rising.
examine_details.tower_four: Tower 4 is visible up the slope. Something is wrong with it — the crossarm is tilted. The cable is riding on the edge of the sheave, not in the groove.
examine_details.summit_view: The summit of {city_name} Peak. Beautiful. Dozens of people are up there, waiting for the lift to bring them down.

base.name: High Ground
base.description: The safest point. The place you end up when everything below falls apart.

natural_disaster+geological_event.name: Hilltop Park — Open Ground
natural_disaster+geological_event.description: You sprinted to the center of the park, away from the trees, away from the power lines.
natural_disaster+geological_event.first_entry_text:
[color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] seized you by the collar and made you sprint toward the center of the park, screaming about [color=#FF3131]'the bones of the city snapping.'[/color] You reached the grass just as the air seemed to [color=#aaaaaa]curdle[/color] with a low-frequency hum.

The P-wave hit first—a sudden, [color=#FF3131]violent jolt[/color] that traveled up through your marrow and knocked you to your knees. Then, the S-wave arrived, and the world became [color=#55aaff]liquid[/color]. The solid earth began to roll in nauseating, five-foot swells like the surface of a [color=#55aaff]storm-tossed ocean[/color].

You watched as the brick facade of the library across the street began to [color=#aaaaaa]peel away[/color] from its steel skeleton. It didn't just fall; the masonry [color=#FF3131]disintegrated[/color] into a spray of red dust before the weight of the roof hammered the cars below into [color=#aaaaaa]scrap metal[/color]. The sound is a [color=#FF3131]deafening, planetary grinding[/color] mixed with the [color=#aaaaaa]shriek of unzipping rebar[/color] and the distance-muffled screams of the trapped.

The earth [color=#FF3131]rips open[/color] along the park path—a jagged, [color=#aaaaaa]hungry fissure[/color] that unzips the soil with a sound like a thousand dry branches breaking at once. It expands rapidly toward your feet, the abyss showing only [color=#55aaff]churning mud and broken pipes[/color], before it finally halts.

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] You stand in the silence of the settling dust, looking at the [color=#FF3131]pulverized horizon[/color]. [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] felt the planet's teeth shift before the first stone moved. You were supposed to be [color=#FF3131]swallowed[/color] by that street.

examine_details.fissure: A jagged crack in the earth, easily three feet wide and growing.
examine_details.collapsing_library: A cloud of brick dust obscures the street. The aftershocks are already starting.

winter_sports+mechanical_failure.name: {city_name} Peak — Treeline Clearing
winter_sports+mechanical_failure.description: A clearing in the trees above the mid-station. You hiked up instead of taking the lift. You're not sure why you believed them.
winter_sports+mechanical_failure.first_entry_text:
You were still wondering if you fully believed [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] when they suddenly bolted from the lift line and scrambled up the adjacent hiking trail. You followed them.

[color=#5eafd2]Then, the main cable makes a sound.[/color]

Not the usual steady, mechanical hum. It's a harmonic —a high, singing vibration, rising in pitch until it sets your teeth on edge.

With a sound like a cannon shot, [color=#BC13FE]Tower 4's massive steel crossarm shears clean off[/color].

The tension releases in a violent whip. The cable lashes out, shearing through fifty-foot pines on both sides of the line. The gondolas plummet, [color=#5eafd2]but they don't hit the snow[/color]—they pendulum wildly out over the mountain pass road below, right into the path of a transit bus that had left from the lodge.

A giant capsule of steel and glass slams into it broadside - [color=#5eafd2]the impact yanking the massive lift cable taut[/color]. Tires skidding desperately on the black ice, the bus is violently dragged off course. It tips and rolls heavily down the steep grade, [color=#BC13FE]wrapping itself in the snapped cable like a horrific spool[/color].

The tumbling bus acts as a massive anchor, dragging the entire lift system down with it. [color=#5eafd2]Towers buckle in a catastrophic domino effect.[/color] More gondolas are ripped from the sky, smashing into the lodge as the cable begins [color=#BC13FE]sawing through the patio supports[/color] as the crowd above looks on in absolute, frozen horror.

The mountain echoes with a chorus of tearing metal and screaming steel.

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color][color=#BC13FE] {visionary}[/color] was actually right.

examine_details.fallen_gondolas: Gondolas lie scattered down the slope. Some are crumpled against the trees. Others are half-buried in the snow. You can count eight from here. The lift was carrying forty.
examine_details.snapped_cable: The steel cable lies across the snow in a massive arc, still under tension where it's caught on Tower 3. The trees it cut through are still falling.

natural_disaster+coastal_disaster.name: Coastal Bluff — Storm Shelter Ruins
natural_disaster+coastal_disaster.description: The concrete shelter at the top of the coastal bluff. Its roof is gone — took by a previous storm years ago. The walls are still standing.
natural_disaster+coastal_disaster.first_entry_text:
The forecast predicted a Category 2. It slammed into the coast as a [color=#FF3131]screaming Category 4[/color].

You were on the boardwalk when the first band hit, and the air turned into a [color=#aaaaaa]shrieking, solid weight[/color]. The wind was already wrong—not gusting, but a sustained, [color=#FF3131]predatory pressure[/color] that leaned against your ribs until you couldn't breathe. 

[color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] seized you, their fingers bruising your arm, and dragged you up the bluff path toward the old shelter, screaming about the [color=#aaaaaa]'wall of water headed for us.'[/color]

From the ridge, you see the full, [color=#FF3131]apocalyptic sweep[/color] of the coastline. The storm surge is coming in now—it isn't waves; it’s a [color=#55aaff]dark, silt-choked wall of the abyss[/color]. It is a continuous, rising elevation of the sea level, advancing with a [color=#aaaaaa]low, rhythmic thunder[/color]. The boardwalk doesn't just flood; it is [color=#FF3131]pulverized and swallowed[/color] in thirty seconds. The parking lot vanishes in two minutes.

Then, the sound of [color=#aaaaaa]shearing bolts[/color]. The buildings that aren't anchored to the bedrock begin to drift, rotating like toy boats before the currents [color=#FF3131]hammer them into toothpicks[/color] against the bluff.

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] felt the barometric pressure drop in their marrow. You were supposed to be [color=#FF3131]drowned and shredded[/color] in that surf.

examine_details.surge_panorama: The coastline below is no longer coastline. It's sea. The storm surge has advanced three blocks inland. Buildings are standing in water to their second floors.
examine_details.shelter_wall: The windward wall of the shelter is flexing — you can feel it vibrating under your hands. The wind speed up here is doing things you didn't think wind could do.

geological_event+urban_disaster.name: {city_name} Business District — Parking Structure Roof
geological_event+urban_disaster.description: The open roof of the parking structure adjacent to the business district. You came up here on your lunch break. You weren't hungry.
geological_event+urban_disaster.first_entry_text:
[color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] seized you by the collar, their breath smelling of [color=#FF3131]adrenaline and iron[/color], and dragged you up the concrete stairs of the parking structure. They wouldn't let you stay on the street—they kept screaming about [color=#aaaaaa]'the ground turning into a mouth.'[/color]

The section of pavement you were standing on thirty seconds ago [color=#FF3131]no longer exists[/color].

It didn't crack or settle with a warning. It [color=#aaaaaa]inverted[/color]—the center simply vanished into a black void, and the asphalt edges curled downward like [color=#FF3131]melting wax[/color]. There was no explosion, only the [color=#aaaaaa]sickening, wet slurp[/color] of a thousand tons of earth being swallowed at once.

The sinkhole is a [color=#FF3131]growing, hungry circle[/color]. From this height, you can see the cause: a massive, [color=#55aaff]corroded water main[/color] that has been scouring the soil into a slurry for years. The road is a hollow shell, and the weight of the world is finally breaking it. 

A food cart. Two parked cars. A bus stop shelter. [color=#FF3131]A person reaching for a door handle[/color]. 

They don't fall; they are [color=#FF3131]erased[/color]. Plunged into a subterranean grave of [color=#55aaff]churning mud and broken concrete[/color].

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] felt the vibration in the soles of their feet before the first pebble dropped. You were supposed to be [color=#FF3131]suffocated[/color] in that dark.

examine_details.sinkhole_panorama: The sinkhole is approximately eighty feet across and still widening at its edges. The depth is unclear — the bottom isn't visible. Utility lines are dangling into it from all sides.
examine_details.subsidence_crack: A crack has propagated from the sinkhole along the street toward the parking structure you're standing on. It's about four inches wide. It wasn't there when you came up here.

natural_disaster+fire_related.name: {city_name} National Forest — Fire Road Crest
natural_disaster+fire_related.description: The crest of the fire road, above the smoke line. You can breathe up here. Barely.
natural_disaster+fire_related.first_entry_text:
[color=#aaaaaa]The smoke descended the ridge like a heavy, suffocating fog.[/color] It wasn't drifting; it was rolling in [color=#FF3131]thick, oily waves[/color] that tasted of [color=#FF3131]charred pine and panic[/color].

You’d been hiking the loop trail when [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] erupted from the undergrowth—not running, but [color=#FF3131]sprinting[/color] with a frantic, animal energy, their eyes streaming and [color=#FF3131]bloodshot from the heat[/color]. They seized your pack and [color=#FF3131]hauled you up the vertical slope[/color] toward the fire road, screaming that the car park was already a [color=#FF3131]chimney[/color].

The crown fire crested the ridge two minutes later. It was a [color=#FF3131]sixty-foot wall of incandescent fury[/color] leaping through the canopy at twenty miles an hour. The sound was a [color=#aaaaaa]physical blow[/color]—not the crackle of a campfire, but a [color=#FF3131]deafening, low-frequency roar[/color] like a jet engine anchored to the earth, [color=#aaaaaa]shredding the oxygen[/color] out of the air.

You watch as the heat front arrives before the flames, the trees in the valley below [color=#FF3131]spontaneously combusting[/color] in a flash of blinding light. Your car—your only way out—is down there, melting into a [color=#aaaaaa]puddle of alloy and glass[/color].

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] knew the wind was a lie. They heard the fire [color=#aaaaaa]breathing[/color] before the first spark jumped the line. You were supposed to be [color=#FF3131]incinerated[/color] in that basin.

examine_details.fire_front_view: The crown fire has burned through the area below the fire road and is moving east. The trees are burning from the tops down. The forest floor beneath them is already ash.
examine_details.smoke_column: The smoke column is visible for thirty miles in every direction, according to the ranger on the radio. They are classifying it as a megafire. That word is new.

man_made_disaster+flood_related.name: {city_name} Valley — Ridge Above the River
man_made_disaster+flood_related.description: The ridge trail above the river bend. You ran here. Your lungs are burning. You can see the full valley floor from this elevation.
man_made_disaster+flood_related.first_entry_text:
You were at the picnic site, the air still and heavy, when the siren shrieked from somewhere far upstream. Not an alarm—a [color=#aaaaaa]sustained, mechanical scream[/color] of a horn. Then silence. Then [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] was on top of you, their fingers digging into your shoulders as they hollered to run uphill—not toward the road, [color=#FF3131]UPHILL, now, NOW[/color].

You ran until your lungs tasted like [color=#FF3131]old iron and panic[/color].

The wave rounded the river bend before you even reached the ridge. It wasn't just water; it was a fifteen-foot [color=#aaaaaa]wall of liquid concrete[/color], moving at highway speed and carrying full-grown trees like [color=#FF3131]splintered chopsticks[/color]. You watch, paralyzed, as a massive oak is hammered through the side of a stationary RV like a [color=#FF3131]harpoon[/color].

The picnic site. The campground. The road. They don't just flood; they [color=#FF3131]disintegrate[/color]. In ninety seconds, the valley floor is erased, replaced by a [color=#55aaff]roaring, silt-choked torrent[/color] that grinds boulders together with a sound like [color=#aaaaaa]shattering bone[/color].

The valley floor is a river now, edge to edge—a graveyard of [color=#FF3131]swirling wreckage[/color] and uprooted lives.

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] God... [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] saw the watermark before the dam even cracked. You were supposed to be [color=#FF3131]pulverized[/color] in that current.

examine_details.valley_flood: The entire valley floor is underwater. The flood line is moving up the ridge below you — still rising. The current at the valley centre is fast enough to carry cars. You've seen three.
examine_details.debris_wave: The debris front of the flood is just below you — a grinding mass of trees, timber, vehicles, and soil. It sounds like a continuous landslide.

infrastructure_failure+flood_related.name: {city_name} Valley — Ridge Above the River
infrastructure_failure+flood_related.description: The ridge trail above the river bend. The valley below looks wrong from here. The river is moving wrong.
infrastructure_failure+flood_related.first_entry_text:
You were at the picnic site, the air still and heavy, when the siren shrieked from upstream. One sustained, [color=#aaaaaa]mechanical scream[/color]. Then silence. Then [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] was already running—already past you—their hands clawing at the air as they pointed toward the high ridges.

You ran until your lungs burned with the [color=#FF3131]scent of wet earth and old iron[/color].

The flood wave rounded the river bend while you were still scrambling up the shale. It wasn't just water; it was a fifteen-foot [color=#aaaaaa]wall of liquid concrete[/color] moving at highway speed, churning with the [color=#FF3131]mangled remains[/color] of everything it had already swallowed. You watch as a massive oak tree is snapped like a toothpick and [color=#FF3131]hammered[/color] through the side of a stationary RV.

The picnic site. The campground. The road. The car park. They don't just flood; they [color=#FF3131]disintegrate[/color]. In ninety seconds, the valley floor is erased, replaced by a [color=#55aaff]roaring, silt-choked torrent[/color] that grinds boulders together with a sound like [color=#aaaaaa]shattering bone[/color].

The valley is a river now, edge to edge, a graveyard of [color=#FF3131]swirling wreckage[/color] and uprooted lives.

[color=#FF3131]{reaction}[/color] [color=#BC13FE]{visionary}[/color] saw the watermark before the dam even cracked. You were supposed to be [color=#FF3131]pulverized[/color] in that current.

examine_details.valley_flood: The entire valley is underwater. You can see the flood line still rising on the ridge below you. The current at the centre is fast enough to carry cars. You've watched three go past.
examine_details.debris_wave: The debris front is just below you — trees, timber, vehicles, sections of road surface. The noise is constant, like a landslide that never ends.
